techArticleSchema.headline: Ergo: How Protocol Technologies Connect (7-Minute Map)
techArticleSchema.description: Comprehensive guide to Ergo blockchain architecture showing how eUTXO, ErgoScript, Autolykos, Sigma protocols, and other components interconnect to create a secure, scalable, and sustainable blockchain platform.

about.name: eUTXO Model
about.description: Extended UTXO model enabling smart contracts with UTXO security

about.name: ErgoScript
about.description: Functional smart contract language with built-in cryptographic primitives

about.name: Sigma Protocols
about.description: Zero-knowledge proof system without trusted setup

mainEntity.name: How does eUTXO differ from Bitcoin's UTXO model?

acceptedAnswer.text: eUTXO extends Bitcoin's UTXO with registers (R0-R9) for data storage and ErgoTree guard scripts for programmable spending conditions. This enables smart contracts while maintaining UTXO's security and parallelism benefits.

mainEntity.name: What makes Sigma protocols special for blockchain?

acceptedAnswer.text: Sigma protocols provide zero-knowledge proofs without trusted setup, enabling privacy features like ring signatures and confidential transactions. They're built directly into ErgoScript, making advanced cryptography accessible to developers.

mainEntity.name: Why does Ergo need Storage Rent?

acceptedAnswer.text: Storage Rent prevents blockchain bloat by recycling fees from unused UTXOs back to miners. This ensures long-term sustainability and predictable storage costs, solving the 'state explosion' problem other blockchains face.

mainEntity.name: How do light clients trust the network without full validation?

acceptedAnswer.text: NIPoPoWs (Non-Interactive Proofs of Proof-of-Work) provide cryptographically secure proofs that a transaction is included in the blockchain. Light clients can verify these logarithmic-size proofs without downloading the entire chain.
